paper_title: Roles of MLLMs in Visually Rich Document Retrieval for RAG: A Survey
focus_area: Visually Rich Document (VRD) Retrieval for Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
key_challenges: VRD retrieval requires understanding fusion of layout, embedded text, and graphics
Traditional preprocessing (OCR, layout parsing) is brittle and lossy
Fine-grained visual cues vanish in textual proxies
Evidence may span multiple pages or views

approaches.name: Document Screenshot Embedding (DSE)
approaches.description: Treats page screenshot as indexing unit, avoiding preprocessing errors

approaches.name: ColPali
approaches.description: Fuses Vision-Language Models with late-interaction matching

approaches.name: VisRAG
approaches.description: Integrates vision-based retrieval with generation using page-as-image abstraction

contributions.description: Role-based taxonomy of VRD-RAG organizing MLLM pipeline roles
Comparative analysis of key trade-offs (retrieval unit, robustness, latency, indexing cost, compatibility)
Practical takeaways and open challenges for system design

structure.section_2: Background on RAG, multimodal retrieval, and MLLMs

evaluation_resources: Newer resources for chart reasoning and multi-slide evidence aggregation (Tamber et al., 2025; Yang et al., 2025; Liu et al., 2025; Chen et al., 2025b; Peng et al., 2025)
title: Roles of MLLMs in Visually Rich Document Retrieval for RAG: A Survey
